analysis: Hotspot is OpenMP barrier wait amplified by load imbalance and potential OS thread migration. Selected plan is to reduce runtime variability by enabling consistent OpenMP thread affinity (OMP_PROC_BIND/OMP_PLACES). Safest minimal code change is to set these environment variables only if not already set by the user/job launcher, preserving existing policies and avoiding conflicts. This does not change loop semantics or reduction behavior.
selected_candidate_target: OpenMP thread placement/affinity to reduce barrier wait and variance
applied_changes_summary: Added a small initialization block before the OpenMP parallel loop to set default OMP_PROC_BIND=close and OMP_PLACES=cores only when they are not already defined in the environment (using setenv/getenv). No changes to OpenMP pragmas or computation.
correctness_risks: On some systems, forcing affinity can reduce performance if it conflicts with external pinning/topology expectations (mitigated by only setting when unset).
Non-POSIX platforms without setenv may fail to compile (mitigated by guarding with _OPENMP and providing a no-op fallback when setenv is unavailable).
